question:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.
The robbers were <u>captured</u> and brought to the jail.
explanation:
• **Captured**: Taken into one's possession or control by force. ⛓️
• **Released**: To allow or enable to escape from confinement; set free. (Antonym). 🔓

question:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
She needs to be ________ if she wants to stand up to her bullies.
explanation:
• **Bolder**: (Of a person or action) showing an ability to take risks; confident and courageous. 🦁
• **Boulder**: A large rock. 
• **Balder/Blonder**: Physical descriptions of hair or baldness.

question:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
A couple of parrots __________ on the telephone wires.
explanation: • **Perched**: (Of a bird) alighted or rested on something. 🦜

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom.
The child made a <u>wry face</u> when he was denied entry into the museum.
explanation: • **Make a wry face**: To produce an expression on the face that shows you think something is bad or slightly annoying; a grimace of disappointment or disgust. ☹️

question:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.
**Tasty**
explanation:
• **Tasty**: Having a pleasant, distinct flavor. 🍕
• **Insipid**: Lacking flavor; weak or tasteless. (Antonym). 🍲💨

question:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
A feeling comes over one that so much evil and misfortune _______ the world that a little more or less does not make much difference.
explanation: • **Tense**: The sentence describes a general feeling or timeless truth (Present Tense). 'Misfortune' and 'evil' (plural subject here) take the base form '**shadow**'. ✅

question:
Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.
**Baffle**
explanation: • **Baffle**: Totally bewilder or perplex. (Synonym: Confuse/Puzzle). 🧩

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.
**Be in seventh heaven**
explanation: • **Be in seventh heaven**: To be in a state of extreme happiness or bliss. ☁️😊

question:
Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment.
The doctors always wear a <u>right and suitable</u> uniform.
explanation: • **Contextual substitution**: A uniform that is suitable and correct for the task is a '**fit**' or 'appropriate' uniform. 🏥

question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
explanation:
• **Incorrect spelling**: Defroded. ❌
• **Correct spelling**: **Defrauded**. ✅ (Meaning to illegally obtain money from someone by deception).

question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in **past perfect tense**.
options: Fortunately, the movie had not been starting on time.
Fortunately, the movie was not starting on time.
Fortunately, the movie had not started on time.
Fortunately, the movie did not start on time.
answer: Fortunately, the movie had not started on time.
explanation: • **Past Perfect**: Formed with 'had' + past participle (**started**). Option C is the only correct structure. 🎬🕒

question:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment.
He asked me <u>if I have</u> anything to say.
explanation: • **Reported Speech**: When the reporting verb is in the past ('asked'), the present tense ('have') in the subordinate clause changes to the past tense (**had**). 🗣️

question:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.
**Officious**
explanation:
• **Officious**: Assertive of authority in an annoyingly domineering way, especially with regard to petty or trivial matters. 😠👮
• **Timid**: Showing a lack of courage or confidence; easily frightened. (Antonym). 🐭

question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
explanation:
• **Incorrect spelling**: Conveneince. ❌
• **Correct spelling**: **Convenience**. ✅ (The 'i' comes before 'e' in the suffix). 🛎️

question:
Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.
**Accomplish**
explanation: • **Accomplish**: To achieve or complete successfully. (Synonym: Achieve). 🏆

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 1) Twenty five years ago, few people realised that computers (1)___________ about to become part of our daily lives.
explanation: • **Tense & Agreement**: The sentence refers to the past ('Twenty five years ago') and the subject 'computers' is plural, requiring '**were**'. 💻

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 2) This short period of time has seen (2)__________ changes...
explanation: • **Enormous**: Very large in size, quantity, or extent. The impact of computers has been massive. 📈

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 3) People... are now freed from these (3)_________ tasks.
explanation: • **Boring**: Adjective describing a task that is tedious or uninteresting. 😴

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 4) Some of us may wonder, (4)_________, life has really improved...
explanation: • **Conjunction**: '**However**' is used to introduce a contrasting point or a doubt following the positive points about computers. ⚖️

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 5) Employers complain that clerical (5)________ have become dependent on calculators.
explanation: • **Staff**: All the people employed by a particular organization. 'Clerical staff' refers to office workers. 🏢
